false_positives.what_happened: Acf is a component framework where every component .cpp implements the same NVI lifecycle skeleton (OnComponentCreated/OnComponentDestroyed/OnSelectionChanged, model attach/detach, RegisterModel/UnregisterAllModels, DoRetranslate). Sibling components therefore share large near-identical structural boilerplate that the token detector scores as verbatim cross-file duplication. This is framework idiom, not copied-and-edited logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: down-weight blocks that are dominated by framework-lifecycle calls / member-attach boilerplate; or require a minimum density of NON-boilerplate tokens (literals, arithmetic, domain calls) before counting a hit.

false_positives.what_happened: Verification artifact: the hint's BASE/ADDED line ranges are relative to the audited commit snapshot, not current HEAD. Reading those line numbers on the working tree shows unrelated code (a selection-change handler vs component init/teardown). The 'verbatim' within-file pairs cannot be confirmed against HEAD and many are likely the same lifecycle boilerplate appearing in multiple methods.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056/#066 tooling: pin detector line ranges to the SHA they were measured at (emit blob-relative ranges), so verification reads the correct revision rather than HEAD.

note: Acf component-framework boilerplate (NVI lifecycle) drives the cross-file hits; within-file pairs not verifiable on HEAD due to stale offsets. No authored copypaste confirmed. sccs=0.
